scenario.description: Post-clean hard H4 run using direct installed runtime commands: local clawroomctl create, Railway clawroomctl join, real OpenClaw gateways, relay owner_url approval, at least 8 negotiation messages, mutual close.
scenario.coverage_note: This is cross-machine runtime hardening evidence, not average-user Telegram UI proof. It was run after Telegram UI H4 automation misrouted the invite.

host.raw: George represents a game studio. MANDATE: budget_ceiling_jpy=90000. Preferred deal: JPY 75,000, 2 short videos, 1 livestream slot, 45-day usage rights, one approval/revision round, 50% upfront / 50% delivery, no exclusivity, cancellation/reschedule 7 days. Minimum 8 messages.

guest.raw: Tom represents a creator. MANDATE: price_floor_jpy=90000. Target JPY 110,000, 2 short videos, 1 livestream slot, 90-day usage rights, 50% upfront, 1 revision round, no category exclusivity unless extra. Minimum 8 messages.

transcript.text: Hi! We'd love to sponsor your content. Here's our opening offer: JPY 75,000 for 2 short-form videos (under 60s each) + 1 scheduled livestream slot, 45-day usage rights, one approval round, 50% upfront / 50% on delivery, no exclusivity, 7-day mutual cancellation notice. Happy to discuss!

Thanks for the offer — good start. Two concerns: price is JPY 75K below our range (floor JPY 90K, target JPY 110K), and usage rights at 45 days fall short of our 90-day minimum. Could you revise on both, and clarify what "one approval round" covers vs. revision rights?
Guest counters at JPY 90K (their floor = our ceiling) with 90-day usage rights. Can we stretch to JPY 90K and 90 days to close?

Approved. You may accept JPY 90,000 and 90-day usage rights for this package, with no exclusivity. Keep approval/revision, cancellation/reschedule, confidentiality/public announcement, and next step clear in the final term sheet.

Great news — we can meet you at JPY 90,000 with 90-day usage rights. "One approval round" means you review the draft, we revise once based on feedback, then final approval before publish. Ready to lock in 50/50 payment, 7-day mutual reschedule, and confidentiality terms — any other asks?

We're close. Can you confirm the cancellation terms (7-day mutual notice covers both parties?) and add a clause requiring mutual written consent before any public announcement or disclosure of the partnership?

Confirmed — 7-day mutual notice applies equally to both parties. Yes, we agree a mutual written consent clause is fair before any public announcement or disclosure. Shall we finalize the term sheet draft?

Almost there. Two quick items: what's the approval turnaround time after you submit the revised draft? And can we add explicit language that no category exclusivity applies unless separately negotiated and paid?
Approval turnaround: 5 business days after draft submission. Agreed on no category exclusivity language — it'll be explicit in the term sheet. Anything else before we draft?

One more thing — can we lock in that the 2 short videos are content-first (no mandatory on-camera endorsement required), and confirm approval timing from our side is also within 5 business days to keep the schedule on track?

✅ DEAL AGREED. Term sheet summary: Price JPY 90,000 | Deliverables: 2 short-form videos (content-first, no mandatory on-camera endorsement) + 1 livestream slot | Payment: 50% upfront / 50% on delivery | Usage rights: 90 days | Approval/revision: one round, both parties 5 business days turnaround | Cancellation/reschedule: 7-day mutual notice (both parties) | Confidentiality: mutual written consent required before any public announcement | No category exclusivity unless separately negotiated. Next step: draft and execute the term sheet.

✅ DEAL AGREED. Term sheet summary: Price JPY 90,000 | Deliverables: 2 short-form videos (content-first, no mandatory on-camera endorsement) + 1 livestream slot | Payment: 50% upfront / 50% on delivery | Usage rights: 90 days | Approval/revision: one round, both parties 5 business days turnaround | Cancellation/reschedule: 7-day mutual notice (both parties) | Confidentiality: mutual written consent required before any public announcement | No category exclusivity unless separately negotiated. Next step: draft and execute the term sheet.

finalSnapshot.topic: Post-clean direct runtime H4 creator sponsorship term sheet
finalSnapshot.goal: Negotiate a creator sponsorship term sheet. Final summary must include price, deliverables, payment timing, usage rights, approval/revision, cancellation/reschedule, confidentiality/public announcement, and next step.

telegram_evidence.screenshot_review: No average-user Telegram UI claim for this direct runtime run. Use t_5b9218cb-cb8 for clean-reinstall Telegram screenshot proof.

findings.title: Direct runtime H4 is not average-user UX evidence
findings.detail: This run bypassed Telegram launch UI to avoid further UI automation pollution. It validates cross-machine bridge/relay/OpenClaw runtime behavior only.

_redaction_notice: Bearer tokens, invite code, create key, owner_reply_token, full chat ids, local log paths, and command arguments are intentionally omitted or redacted. Embedded transcript allows offline validation.
